c:\\windows\\system32\\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x50b0   creator process name: c:\\windows\\explorer.exe   process command line: \"c:\\windows\\system32\\rundll32.exe\" c:\\windows\\system32\\shell32.dll,control_rundll c:\\windows\\system32\\srchadmin.dll    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the closed_reason: calling this benign, did some research and it appears that is normal windows activity related to windows search.
description: this rule looks for suspicious rundll32.exe process executions with command lines that invoke control_rundll or shellexec_rundll functions from shell32.dll, a core windows shell dll. attackers leverage this legitimate functionality as a living off the land binary (lolbin) to execute arbitrary code, launch dll payloads, executables, or even command-line arguments, serving as a versatile method for code execution and defense evasion.
message: windows methodology [rundll32 - shell32.dll arbitrary code execution]
